name: Köthe Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The category of locally convex topological vector spaces with continuous linear functionals forming a complete reflexive system

VINIT.justification: Before any Köthe matrix is defined, there is no structure — void state ∅ from which all sequence spaces emerge

TANCH.chosen_element: the Köthe dual (λ-dual) as terminal anchor
TANCH.justification: The dual space closes and contains the entire system; it is the hermetic boundary where all functionals are captured

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the natural injection of a Köthe space into its bidual, ascending toward reflexivity

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: descent from the bidual back to the original space, the coagula step in duality

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: embedding then restricting, or dual then bidual, forming the root→derived morphism chain

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity self-reference: the space recognizes itself in its bidual; the stone that knows itself

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the Köthe matrix A splits into two branches: T-arm (rows satisfy Köthe condition) and F-arm (rows violate it)
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication δ: one matrix branches into two distinct evaluation paths — perfect duality vs. imperfect duality

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of the Köthe matrix from both branches
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication μ: the T-arm (perfect duality) and F-arm (imperfect duality) fuse back into the original matrix structure; FFUSE(FSPLIT(A)) = A

EVALT.chosen_element: the Köthe condition holds: for all i, there exists j such that a(i,j) < ∞ and a(i,j) > 0
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the space is perfect, reflexive, and satisfies the duality theorem

EVALF.chosen_element: the Köthe condition fails: there exists i such that for all j, either a(i,j) = ∞ or a(i,j) = 0
EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the space is imperfect, non-reflexive, duality breaks

ENGAGR.chosen_element: a Köthe space simultaneously satisfying and violating the condition in different coordinate directions
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both perfect and imperfect duality held without resolution — a bicameral compromise in the matrix structure

IFIX.chosen_element: the Köthe matrix A is permanently recorded as a fixed append-only sequence of rows
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once a row is appended to the matrix, it cannot be undone; the measurement record of the space's structure

phase_2.split_input: A (the complete Köthe matrix with all rows and their growth rates)
split_outputs: T-arm: rows satisfying the Köthe condition (perfect duality, reflexivity)
F-arm: rows violating the Köthe condition (imperfect duality, non-reflexivity)
phase_2.fuse_result: A (the original Köthe matrix, fully recovered from both branches)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no Köthe matrix defined; no sequence space structure exists; uninitialized topology
phase_3.true_description: 01 — the Köthe condition holds; the space is perfect, reflexive, and satisfies complete duality; λ(A) ≅ λ(A)′′
phase_3.false_description: 10 — the Köthe condition fails; the space is imperfect, non-reflexive; duality is broken or incomplete
phase_3.both_description: 11 — a paradice: the matrix simultaneously exhibits perfect duality in some coordinates and imperfect duality in others; both states held without resolution

steps.domain_action: Initialize the void — no Köthe matrix, no sequence space structure exists yet
Establish the terminal anchor — the Köthe dual λ(A)′ as the boundary containing all continuous linear functionals
Define the canonical embedding λ(A) → λ(A)′′ — forward morphism toward reflexivity
Branch the Köthe matrix A into two evaluation paths: T-arm (rows satisfying Köthe condition) and F-arm (rows violating it)
Affirm the T-arm — for each row i in the T-arm, there exists j such that 0 < a(i,j) < ∞; perfect duality holds
Compose the T-arm evaluation with the embedding morphism — chain the forward transformation through the perfect duality branch
Recognize reflexivity in the T-arm — λ(A) ≅ λ(A)′′; the space knows itself in its bidual
Negate the F-arm — for each row i in the F-arm, either a(i,j) = ∞ for all j, or a(i,j) = 0 for all j; duality fails
Define the restriction map λ(A)′′ → λ(A) on the F-arm — reverse morphism, descent from bidual
Compose the F-arm evaluation with the restriction morphism — chain the reverse transformation through the imperfect duality branch
Introduce a paradice — a Köthe matrix with mixed rows: some satisfying the condition (T-state), others violating it (F-state), both held simultaneously
Reconstitute the original Köthe matrix A from both branches — the T-arm (perfect duality) and F-arm (imperfect duality) fuse back into A; FFUSE(FSPLIT(A)) = A
Record the Köthe matrix A as a permanent, append-only sequence of rows — each row is fixed in ROM, cannot be undone
Close the loop — the final state recognizes the initial structure; reflexivity is self-referential
Seal the boundary — the Köthe dual λ(A)′ contains and closes the entire system

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Köthe condition evaluator — translates the matrix A into a sequence of row-by-row checks: for each i, does there exist j with 0 < a(i,j) < ∞?
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Duality pairing ⟨·,·⟩: λ(A) × λ(A)′ → ℝ — continuous linear functionals communicate with sequence elements via the bilinear form
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The Köthe matrix A stored as a doubly-indexed array of growth rates a(i,j); each row is an append-only record of coordinate growth bounds
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Row-by-row evaluation order — process rows sequentially; for each row, check the Köthe condition before appending to the permanent record
phase_5.alfs_store: The canonical Köthe space ℓ^p (p-summable sequences) — the bootstrap reference where the theorem is first verified

phase_6.cycle_cost: Per evaluation cycle: one row processed, one Köthe condition check performed, one bit of information (T or F) recorded
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Uninitialized Köthe matrix; no rows evaluated; no duality structure established
phase_6.post_cycle_state: One row appended to the permanent record; duality status (perfect or imperfect) determined for that row; reflexivity state updated
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because the Köthe condition is a deterministic, reversible check: given A and row i, the T/F outcome is uniquely determined; no information is lost; the FSPLIT/FFUSE pair preserves the total structure (FFUSE(FSPLIT(A)) = A)

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → IMSCRIB → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Köthe Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 11)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def k_the_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the void — no Köthe matrix, no sequence space structure exists yet)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Establish the terminal anchor — the Köthe dual λ(A)′ as the boundary containi...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Define the canonical embedding λ(A) → λ(A)′′ — forward morphism toward reflex...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch the Köthe matrix A into two evaluation paths: T-arm (rows satisfying Köthe condition) and F-arm (rows violating it)) / FFUSE [11] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirm the T-arm — for each row i in the T-arm, there exists j such that 0 < ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the T-arm evaluation with the embedding morphism — chain the forward ...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Recognize reflexivity in the T-arm — λ(A) ≅ λ(A)′′; the space knows itself in...)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Negate the F-arm — for each row i in the F-arm, either a(i,j) = ∞ for all j, ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Define the restriction map λ(A)′′ → λ(A) on the F-arm — reverse morphism, des...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the F-arm evaluation with the restriction morphism — chain the revers...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Introduce a paradice — a Köthe matrix with mixed rows: some satisfying the co...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [11]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute the original Köthe matrix A from both branches — the T-arm (perfect duality) and F-arm (imperfect duality) fuse back into A; FFUSE(FSPLIT(A)) = A)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the Köthe matrix A as a permanent, append-only sequence of rows — each...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Close the loop — the final state recognizes the initial structure; reflexivit...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the boundary — the Köthe dual λ(A)′ contains and closes the entire system)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def k_the_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (k_the_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def k_the_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (k_the_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem k_the_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
